_README: Agent-produced facets (Step B). Keyed by items.paper_full_text_path. Each facet is {v, note}: v = canonical value (recurs to connect papers), note = how THIS paper relates to it. _definitions[dimension::value] = what the tag means. Re-run apply-facets.mjs after editing (idempotent). Pilot: HCI & Adaptive Interfaces.

_definitions.ai_technique::Human-AI interaction: Designing and studying how people interact with AI systems as collaborators or tools.
_definitions.ai_technique::Adaptive/intelligent UI: Interfaces (AUI/IUI) that adjust their layout, content, or behaviour to the user, often using AI.
_definitions.ai_technique::User modeling: Building a representation of a user's preferences, abilities, or context to drive personalization.
_definitions.ai_technique::Machine learning: Algorithms that learn patterns from data to power prediction or adaptation.
_definitions.ai_technique::Autonomous agents & automa: Systems that act on the user's behalf with varying degrees of independence.
_definitions.ai_technique::Human-AI co-creation: People and generative AI producing content or designs together, sharing agency.
_definitions.ai_technique::Generative AI: AI that produces new content (text, images, designs) as an active creative collaborator.
_definitions.ux_effect::Participation & collaboration: Designing with and among users and stakeholders rather than only for them.
_definitions.ux_effect::User engagement: How strongly an experience draws users in and sustains their attention and interest.
_definitions.ux_effect::Wayfinding & spatial orientat: How people locate themselves and navigate within physical or virtual space.
_definitions.ux_effect::Sense of place: The felt, qualitative character and meaning a person attaches to a space.
_definitions.ux_effect::Personalization & adaptation: Tailoring the experience to an individual user's needs, context, or behaviour.
_definitions.ux_effect::Usability: How easily and effectively users can accomplish their tasks with a system.
_definitions.ux_effect::Immersion & presence: The degree to which a (often virtual) environment makes the user feel 'really there'.
_definitions.ux_effect::Emotional response: The feelings a design or place evokes in the user.
_definitions.ux_effect::Experiential learning: Learning through direct, often immersive, first-hand experience.
_definitions.ux_effect::Behaviour change: Designing technology to shift users' habits or actions over time.
_definitions.ux_effect::Trust: A user's willingness to rely on a system under uncertainty.
_definitions.challenge::Stakeholder collaboration: Coordinating the differing needs of users, communities, and decision-makers.
_definitions.challenge::Adapting to diverse users: Serving users who differ in ability, culture, context, or expertise.
_definitions.challenge::Measuring user experience: Capturing subjective, multi-faceted experience in valid, comparable ways.
_definitions.challenge::Disorientation: Users losing their sense of position or direction in a space or interface.
_definitions.challenge::Communicating intangible/sens: Conveying the felt, sensory, or qualitative aspects of a place or experience.
_definitions.challenge::Fragmented research: A scattered, under-consolidated body of work that is hard to synthesize.
_definitions.challenge::AI unpredictability: AI systems making unexpected or hard-to-anticipate errors.
_definitions.challenge::Designing for uncertainty: Designing when a system's capabilities or outputs are not fully predictable.
_definitions.challenge::Motion sickness: Discomfort (nausea, disorientation) induced by immersive VR/AR.
_definitions.metric::Qualitative interviews: Insights gathered from semi-structured interviews or open feedback.
_definitions.metric::Survey responses: Data collected from questionnaires across many respondents.
_definitions.metric::Wayfinding performance: Objective measures of navigation success (time, errors, route).
_definitions.metric::Behavioural measures: Observed actions and behaviour used as evidence, rather than self-report.
_definitions.metric::Usability evaluation: Assessing ease-of-use via tasks, heuristics, or usability testing.
_definitions.metric::Presence rating: Self-reported sense of 'being there' in a virtual environment.
_definitions.metric::Ownership & realism: Felt body-ownership and perceived realism of a virtual experience.
_definitions.metric::Engagement metrics: Indicators of enjoyment, attention, or sustained involvement.
_definitions.metric::Self-report measures: Data from users reporting their own attitudes or states (e.g. questionnaires).
_definitions.metric::Physiological measures: Bodily signals (e.g. heart rate, gaze) used as objective indicators.
_definitions.ethical_concern::User empowerment: Giving users genuine voice, control, and benefit in the system.
_definitions.ethical_concern::User autonomy & agency: Preserving the user's capacity to decide and act for themselves.
_definitions.ethical_concern::Digital divide: Unequal access to technology across people and groups.
_definitions.ethical_concern::Societal impact of AI: Broader, sometimes harmful, effects of AI on society.
_definitions.ethical_concern::Inclusion & accessibili: Ensuring a system works for the widest possible range of people.
_definitions.ethical_concern::Behaviour manipulation: The risk of nudging or persuading users in ways that may not serve them.

uxEffects.note: The seminal UTOPIA project — workers, unions, and researchers co-designing workplace IT.
Sustained, active involvement of workplace users throughout development.

challenges.note: Aligns workers, trade unions, and researchers in the Scandinavian tradition.
Confronts broadening use contexts beyond the original workplace.

ethicalConcerns.note: Centers worker voice and control over the technology shaping their work.

uxEffects.note: A museum web app designed explicitly to grow audience engagement.
Human-centred design framework aimed at an easy, useful museum site.
Stakeholder interviews and personas shape a co-designed plan.

challenges.note: Gauges the experience through observation sessions and testing.

metrics.note: Five stakeholder interviews and four observation sessions.

uxEffects.note: How residents vs. strangers orient using reference points.
Reference points give a city legibility and meaning.

challenges.note: Poor visual readability leaves people unable to orient.

uxEffects.note: 'Interactive architecture' adapts the design to all factors and parameters.
A multi-relational process engaging many aspects of design.

challenges.note: Seeks optimum adaption across many competing design factors.

uxEffects.note: Evaluates wayfinding behaviour in complex buildings.
A user-centred approach to building usability.

challenges.note: Tackles the feeling of being 'lost' in unfamiliar buildings.
Empirically evaluates UX alongside wayfinding behaviour.

metrics.note: Measures navigation behaviour in architectural environments.
Observes how people actually move and find their way.

uxEffects.note: Surveys VR/AR systems that immerse designers in the design.
Proposes CORAULIS for hands-on, pedagogical design.
Supports collaborative design among designers, laymen, and decision-makers.

challenges.note: Weighs device advantages and limits for varied users.

uxEffects.note: Participatory urban planning via community emotional mapping.
Maps citizens' emotions about urban space.
Captures how places feel — attractiveness, safety.

challenges.note: Feeds 2,117 residents' input into the city's strategic plan.

uxEffects.note: Uses VR to visualize urban transformation.
Brings citizen input and feedback to the forefront.
Debates future visions of the livable city.

challenges.note: Visualizing urbanism's transformation for stakeholders.

ethicalConcerns.note: Asserts citizen voice in shaping the city.

uxEffects.note: The neuroscience of how architecture and urban space are experienced.
How built environments affect people neurologically and emotionally.

challenges.note: Bridges neuroscience and the felt experience of space.

uxEffects.note: An immersive VR prototype for experiencing urban places.
Foregrounds the sensory, intangible aspects of place.
Multi-sensory soundscapes evoke the feel of a place.

challenges.note: Conveys sensory/qualitative place aspects that resist text or graphs.
Seeks valid evaluation of the immersive approach.

metrics.note: Feedback gathered from participants and observers.

uxEffects.note: VR places students inside urban-design scenarios.
VR as an active, participative educational methodology.
Architecture students and professionals take part.

challenges.note: Fits tech-savvy learners across varied subjects.

uxEffects.note: A series for sharing ideas across design fields.

challenges.note: Attempts to map a varied, scattered design-research landscape.

uxEffects.note: Cyberspace as a new, transposed virtual experience of the city.
The 'digital city' reframes the lived experience of space.

challenges.note: Digitalizes experience beyond physical and sensory interaction.

ethicalConcerns.note: Notes its use is not available to everyone.
Raises the ethics of mediated, digitalized experience.

aiTechniques.note: Examines why human-AI interaction is uniquely hard to design.

uxEffects.note: AI's adaptive, complex output shapes the user experience.

challenges.note: AI makes unpredictable errors that damage UX.
Uncertainty around AI's capabilities and output complexity.

ethicalConcerns.note: AI errors can lead to undesired societal impact.

aiTechniques.note: Lays out the foundations of adaptive/intelligent UIs (AUI/IUI).
Building an accurate user preference model is key.
ML training and convergence underpin adaptive interfaces.

uxEffects.note: Reviews universal usability across 165 papers.
Plasticity — interfaces that adapt to users and contexts.

challenges.note: Interfaces must serve users of varied abilities and cultures.
Synthesizes 55 years of scattered work into a taxonomy.

metrics.note: Assesses the plasticity and usability of adaptive UIs.

ethicalConcerns.note: 'Universal usability' — interfaces that work for everyone.

uxEffects.note: AR overlays immersive interaction onto the city.
Human-city interaction within connected smart cities.

challenges.note: Reviews 260 articles and flags under-explored issues.
Constrained interfaces and input across many contexts.

uxEffects.note: Behavioural Intervention Technologies (BITs) to shift user behaviour.
Human-centred BITs tailored to behaviour-change processes.

challenges.note: Consolidates 28 scattered publications into a synthesis.

ethicalConcerns.note: Designing technology to deliberately change behaviour.
Raises who controls the intended behaviour change.

uxEffects.note: Full-immersion VR for situated experiential education.
VR stands in for declining residential fieldtrips.

challenges.note: Motion sickness emerged as a key negative experience.
Applies a three-facet UX analytical frame.

metrics.note: Semi-structured interviews with staff and students.
Assesses the felt sense of the VR experience.

aiTechniques.note: Concerns trust in automated and autonomous agents.

uxEffects.note: A comprehensive review of Trust in Automation (TiA).

challenges.note: How to validly capture the complex trust construct.

uxEffects.note: Hand-tracking and grasping visualizations for immersive VR.

challenges.note: Tests whether lab effects hold in a real game.

metrics.note: Measures presence in a VR escape-room game.

uxEffects.note: A UX-driven maker office space designed to engage users.
Identifies design points for a usable office space.
Atmosphere creation for the office experience.

challenges.note: Analyzes user demand and experience to guide design.

aiTechniques.note: A scoping review of human-AI co-creation through the lens of agency.
Treats AI as an active creative collaborator.

uxEffects.note: Maps control mechanisms: input, action, output, feedback.
Studies how agency is shared in co-creation.

challenges.note: Configurations of agency and control are still unsettled.
Synthesizes 134 papers over 20 years into one framework.

ethicalConcerns.note: Centers the distribution and dynamic of human agency.

uxEffects.note: The character and life of urban streets and squares.
Public-space structure and how people move through it.
